text.title: Design Token Rules and Antipatterns
text.summary: Actionable rules for token generation, contrast, versioning, and common failure modes.

section.title: Generation Rules

rule.statement: Generate CSS custom properties, JS constants, Tailwind config, and platform-native constants from the token JSON via Style Dictionary. Never hand-write generated files.
rule.rationale: Hand-written outputs drift from the source JSON within weeks. Style Dictionary build as a pre-commit hook keeps outputs always in sync.

rule.statement: Make Style Dictionary build a pre-commit hook. Any token JSON edit that does not regenerate outputs must fail CI.
rule.rationale: Agents edit token JSON and forget to rebuild outputs, shipping inconsistent platform CSS and JS.

section.reference: Style Dictionary pipeline emitting CSS vars, JS ES6, and iOS Swift

rule.statement: Run WCAG 2.2 AA contrast checks in CI for every semantic text-on-background pairing. Block PRs that drop any pairing below 4.5:1 for normal text or 3:1 for large text.
rule.rationale: Tokens do not enforce contrast — agents compose text-primary on bg-secondary without checking ratios. CI must be the gate.

rule.statement: Require both light and dark semantic tokens for every new semantic key. CI must fail if a key exists in semantic.json but is absent from semantic-dark.json.
rule.rationale: Dark-mode parity gaps produce invisible text or broken UI in dark mode that only shows up in production.

rule.statement: Mark deprecated tokens with "$deprecated": "use color.bg.primary" in JSON. Linters must reject new usages while allowing old code to compile. Provide migration guides for major version bumps.
rule.rationale: Without deprecation markers, dead tokens accumulate silently and no one knows which are safe to remove.

rule.statement: Add a "search before add" rule: before creating a new token, grep the existing JSON for a token with equivalent purpose. Token count above ~200 requires a lifecycle process (deprecate/merge/split).
rule.rationale: Agents create new tokens instead of reusing, causing token explosion where no one can grep the design vocabulary.

antipattern.description: Hardcoding raw hex values or pixel sizes in component code instead of referencing tokens.
antipattern.reason: Hardcoded values are invisible to the token pipeline. Brand changes require grep-and-replace across all components.

antipattern.description: Flat token structure without hierarchy (one level of keys, no primitive/semantic split).
antipattern.reason: Theming becomes impossible: every consumer must be updated individually instead of changing a semantic alias.

antipattern.description: Syncing tokens by hand between Figma and code (copy-pasting hex values).
antipattern.reason: Manual sync rots within days. Figma should be a view onto the JSON, not the source of truth.

antipattern.description: Agents writing bg-[#3b82f6] arbitrary Tailwind values instead of token-backed utilities.
antipattern.reason: Arbitrary values bypass the token pipeline entirely. Lint with eslint-plugin-tailwindcss no-custom-classname.

antipattern.description: Changing a primitive value to fix a single component without reviewing all consumers.
antipattern.reason: Primitives are shared. Changing color-blue-500 may break N other semantic tokens referencing it.
